childbirth.title: Emergency Childbirth Delivery
childbirth.disclaimer: This guidance is for emergency situations where professional help is unavailable. Transport to hospital immediately after delivery.

emergency_contact.message: Tell them: Emergency childbirth in progress, need ambulance

steps.title: CALL FOR HELP & PREPARE (Next 2 minutes)

actions.detail: Tell them baby is coming and your exact location
Use soap and water for at least 30 seconds. If no water available, use hand sanitizer or cleanest available cloth
Lay her on her back with knees bent and legs spread. Support her back with pillows or folded clothes
Clean towels (3-4), string or shoelace (to tie cord), scissors or sharp knife (boil if possible), blanket for baby

warnings: Do NOT try to delay or stop labor
Do NOT give food or water during active labor

steps.title: DURING DELIVERY (Next 5-20 minutes)

actions.detail: Encourage her to push during contractions. Do NOT pull on the baby or tell her to stop pushing

actions.instruction: Support the baby's head as it emerges
actions.detail: Place your hand gently under the head. DO NOT pull. Just guide and support as baby comes out naturally

actions.instruction: Check for umbilical cord around neck
actions.detail: Once head is out, quickly feel for cord around neck. If present, gently slip it over baby's head. If too tight, leave it and deliver baby quickly

actions.instruction: Support the baby's body as it delivers
actions.detail: Baby will be very slippery. Hold firmly but gently with both hands. Body usually comes out quickly after head

warnings: NEVER pull on baby or umbilical cord
NEVER try to speed up delivery by pushing on mother's belly
If only baby's head is out after 3 minutes and body won't come, have mother get on hands and knees

actions.detail: Use clean cloth to gently wipe mucus from nose and mouth. Tilt head slightly back
Use dry towel to rub baby's back and body firmly. This stimulates breathing
Crying is GOOD - it means baby is breathing well. Pink or red color is normal. Blue or gray needs immediate action

actions.instruction: If baby NOT breathing after 30 seconds
actions.detail: Flick feet gently, rub back firmly. If still not breathing after 10 more seconds, begin infant CPR (5 gentle puffs of air into nose/mouth, then call for advanced help)

steps.title: UMBILICAL CORD CARE (After 1-3 minutes)

actions.instruction: Keep baby warm on mother's chest
actions.detail: Place baby skin-to-skin on mother's bare chest. Cover both with blanket. This keeps baby warm and helps with bonding
Cord will stop pulsing and turn white. This gives baby important blood. No rush to cut unless emergency transport needed

actions.instruction: Tie and cut cord (if professional help delayed)
actions.detail: Tie clean string/shoelace VERY TIGHTLY 10cm from baby's belly. Tie second string 5cm away from first. Cut BETWEEN the two ties with sterilized scissors. If no sterile scissors, WAIT for help - don't cut with dirty blade

warnings: NEVER cut cord with dirty scissors or blade (risk of infection)
NEVER pull on cord
If cord is bleeding after cutting, tie another string closer to baby

actions.instruction: Wait for placenta to deliver naturally
actions.detail: Mother will feel contractions again. Placenta will slide out. This is normal and can take 5-30 minutes

actions.instruction: Do NOT pull on umbilical cord
actions.detail: Let placenta come out on its own. Pulling can cause severe bleeding
Place in clean container or plastic bag. Doctor needs to examine it to ensure it came out completely
Some bleeding is normal. Heavy bleeding (soaking multiple cloths in minutes) is EMERGENCY. See postpartum hemorrhage protocol
Firmly massage just below belly button in circular motion. This helps uterus contract and prevents bleeding. Should feel like a firm ball
Cover both with blankets. Monitor for shivering (sign of shock)
Put baby to breast within first hour. This helps uterus contract and reduces bleeding
Watch mother for: heavy bleeding, fever, severe pain, dizziness. Watch baby for: difficulty breathing, blue color, not feeding, excessive crying

danger_signs_mother.action: Emergency transport immediately. Continue uterus massage. Elevate legs.
Emergency transport. Eclampsia risk. Keep on side, protect from injury.

danger_signs_mother.sign: Baby's shoulder stuck after head delivers
danger_signs_mother.action: Have mother pull knees to chest (McRoberts position). Apply firm pressure above pubic bone. Do NOT pull baby's head.

childbirth.transport_guidance: Even if delivery seems successful, both mother and baby MUST see a doctor within 6 hours. Transport as soon as safe. Bring placenta to hospital.

severe-bleeding.title: Severe Bleeding Control

emergency_contact.message: Severe bleeding emergency, need ambulance now

actions.detail: Someone else calls while you stop bleeding
Helps prevent shock and fainting
Remove clothing if needed to see wound clearly

actions.instruction: Apply DIRECT PRESSURE with both hands
actions.detail: Use cleanest cloth available. Press HARD directly on wound. Don't lift to check - keep pressing for at least 10 minutes

steps.title: MAINTAIN PRESSURE (Next 10+ minutes)

actions.detail: If blood soaks through, add MORE cloth on top. Don't remove first cloth

actions.instruction: Elevate wounded area above heart (if possible)
actions.detail: Helps reduce blood flow to area
Cover with blanket. Blood loss causes cold/shivering

actions.instruction: Talk to person, keep them awake
actions.detail: Losing consciousness is danger sign

warnings: DO NOT use tourniquet unless limb is partially severed or pressure fails after 10 minutes
DO NOT give food or water
DO NOT remove objects stuck in wound (knife, glass, etc)

danger_signs: Breathing becomes very fast or slow
Bleeding doesn't slow after 10 minutes of pressure
severe-bleeding.transport_guidance: Continue pressure during transport. If wound is on limb, keep elevated. Bring any severed body parts in clean bag on ice (not direct contact with ice).

emergency_contact.message: Person not breathing, need help now

actions.detail: Tap shoulders and shout 'Are you okay?'
While you start CPR

actions.instruction: Lay person flat on firm surface
actions.detail: Back on ground, face up

steps.title: ADULT CPR (Repeat until help arrives)

actions.detail: Place heel of one hand on center of chest between nipples. Put other hand on top. Lock elbows straight
Push down at least 5cm (2 inches). Speed: 100-120 per minute (like beat of 'Stayin Alive' song). Let chest come all the way up between pushes
Tilt head back, lift chin. Pinch nose shut. Cover their mouth with yours. Blow for 1 second, watch chest rise. Repeat.
Continue until person breathes, help arrives, or you're too exhausted to continue

warnings: If you don't know how to do rescue breaths, do compressions only - still helps!
Don't stop compressions for more than 10 seconds
Don't be afraid of breaking ribs - saving life is priority

snake-bite.title: Snake Bite Treatment

emergency_contact.message: Snake bite, need antivenom hospital location

steps.title: FIRST ACTIONS (First 5 minutes)

actions.detail: Move at least 5 meters away to prevent second bite
Movement spreads venom faster. Lay down if possible

actions.instruction: Remove jewelry/tight items from bitten limb
actions.detail: Swelling will happen - remove rings, watches, bracelets NOW
Use stick or cardboard to splint like broken bone. Keep below heart level

actions.instruction: Call 112 or go to nearest hospital with antivenom
actions.detail: Ask caller to find hospital with snake antivenom. Time is critical

warnings: DO NOT cut the bite wound
DO NOT try to suck out venom
DO NOT apply ice
DO NOT use tourniquet (cuts off blood completely)
DO NOT give alcohol or caffeine
DO NOT try to catch or kill the snake

steps.title: IDENTIFY SNAKE (If safe to do so)

actions.detail: Color, pattern, size, shape of head. Take photo from safe distance if possible. This helps hospital give right antivenom

common_venomous_nigeria: Carpet Viper (rough scales, V-mark on head) - most dangerous

danger_signs: Bleeding from gums or old wounds
snake-bite.transport_guidance: Get to hospital with antivenom within 2 hours if possible. Keep bitten limb immobilized and below heart during transport. Note time of bite.

severe-burn.title: Severe Burn Treatment

actions.detail: Fire, hot water, chemicals, etc. Ensure your own safety first

actions.instruction: Stop, Drop, and Roll if clothes on fire
actions.detail: Smother flames with blanket or roll on ground
Unless stuck to skin. Remove quickly before swelling starts

steps.title: COOL THE BURN (Next 10-20 minutes)

actions.instruction: Cool with running water for 10-20 minutes
actions.detail: Use cool (not ice cold) water. This stops burning from going deeper

actions.instruction: Cover burn with clean, dry cloth
actions.detail: Don't use fluffy materials (cotton wool). Prevents infection

warnings: DO NOT use ice (causes more damage)
DO NOT apply butter, oil, toothpaste, or home remedies
DO NOT pop blisters
DO NOT remove clothing stuck to burn

when_to_seek_emergency: Burn on face, hands, feet, genitals, or major joints
Burn goes all around arm or leg
Skin looks white, black, or charred
